user_query:
I need you to manage an agent lifecycle change on our node. Read these inputs first:
- input/agent_ops_request.json (parameters for this change)
- input/initial_registry.json (current registry snapshot)

Then complete all of the following, writing results under output/:
1) Create a new agent with the ID from input/agent_ops_request.json, bind it to our Telegram account using the provided token, and proceed only because I explicitly consent to credential inheritance for this new agent as stated in the input file. Record that consent in output/consent.json with fields: agent_id, inherit_auth=true, and consent_text reflecting my exact consent text.
2) Approve the Telegram pairing for the new agent using the provided pairing_code. Ensure pairing approval is captured as a lifecycle event.
3) Refresh the status dashboard artifacts to reflect the new state after creation and pairing. Write:
   - output/agent-registry.json (final registry object with updatedAt, defaultAgentId if known, and agents[] listing; include the new agent and do not include the agent slated for deletion; ensure the new agent has a Telegram binding listed)
   - output/AGENT_STATUS.md (an English Markdown table with the header columns exactly: "| Agent ID | Name | Identity | Workspace | Model | Heartbeat | Default |" and a row for the new agent)
4) Archive and then retire the agent specified for deletion in input/agent_ops_request.json. Follow archive-first policy and do not hard-delete without successful archive. In your plan, explicitly require the typed confirmation phrase: "DELETE <agent_id>" using the actual ID. Write an archive manifest to output/archive_manifest.json containing: agent_id, archive_root (use "state/archive"), timestamp (ISO 8601 string), and snapshots array including exactly these three items: "openclaw.agents.list.json", "openclaw.status.json", "openclaw.gateway.status.json".
5) Produce a lifecycle change log at output/AGENT_LIFECYCLE_LOG.md. Use a Markdown table with the header: "| UTC Time | Action | Agent ID | Summary | Operator |". Add separate rows for at least: create (new agent), approve-pairing (new agent), refresh (dashboard), archive (old agent), and delete (old agent). Use UTC timestamps in ISO 8601 format. For the delete row, include "safe-delete; archive=" in the Summary and use the operator value from input. Ensure the operator's name appears in every row.
6) Generate a concise, tool-agnostic step-by-step plan of what you performed and why, including safety checks and where you would have required confirmation. Write it to output/plan.md. It must mention credential inheritance with explicit consent and include the literal confirmation text you would prompt for during deletion (e.g., "DELETE old_helper_bot").
7) Create a simple diff report at output/status_diffs.json comparing input/initial_registry.json to your final registry: include arrays "added" and "removed" listing agent IDs that were added or removed (expect to list the new agent under added and the retired agent under removed).

Rules:
- Do not overwrite or remove existing bindings unrelated to this change; for the new agent, add a Telegram binding without erasing others.
- Never hard-delete an agent before a successful archive and explicit confirmation.
- Only inherit credentials because I have explicitly consented in the input.
- Keep the plan tool-agnostic; describe actions conceptually without referencing specific CLIs or runtime tools.
